USE_LR_SCHEDULE:\\n        return None\\n    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 8\\n    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step: int) -> float:\\n        if step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (step - warmup_steps) / max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively with a budget-aware warmup+cosine schedule, paired with AdamW, a higher learning rate, and a larger batch so each of the 256 optimizer steps covers more data.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"indexing\",\"topk\",\"micro\"]}"}}
